jasons-whole-wheat-blackberry-cobbler.name: Jason's Whole Wheat Blackberry Cobbler
instructions: Preheat oven to 218°C (220 degrees C). Lightly oil an 8-inch square or 8-inch round baking dish.

Combine blackberries and 1 cup sugar in a saucepan. Whisk warm water and cornstarch together in a bowl until smooth; stir into blackberries. Bring blackberry mixture to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and cook, stirring frequently, until berries break down and thicken, 5 to 10 minutes.

Whisk flour, 1/3 cup sugar, baking powder, and salt together in a bowl. Cut butter into flour mixture using a pastry cutter or your hands until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir milk into flour-butter mixture until a smooth, thick batter forms.
Pour berry mixture into the prepared baking dish. Drop spoonfuls of batter over the berries.
Bake in the preheated oven until a firm and golden crust forms, 30 to 40 minutes.

jerk-chicken-egg-rolls-with-mango-habane.name: Jerk Chicken Egg Rolls With Mango-habanero Sauce
instructions: For the habanero sauce: Combine mango with 3/4 cup water, rice vinegar, garlic, and salt in a small saucepan. Add most of the brown sugar, reserving 1 teaspoon. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low; cook for 5 minutes. Add habanero peppers and simmer for 2 minutes. Mix 2 tablespoon water with cornstarch; stir slurry into the saucepan. Cook over medium-high until thick, 3 to 5 minutes. Leave mixture chunky or blend for a smoother texture.

For the filling: Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add chicken thighs and sauté until browned, 5 to 7 minutes; sprinkle jerk seasoning over chicken. Add shallot and garlic; cook and stir until shallot begins to soften, about 3 minutes. Reduce heat to medium; add cabbage, carrots, and reserved sugar. Cook until cabbage is translucent but still a little crunchy, 3 to 5 minutes. Taste for seasoning. Let mixture cool.
Heat remaining vegetable oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Fry plantains until they start to brown, about 5 minutes. Transfer plantains onto a paper towel and let cool.
Separate and place egg roll wrappers onto your work surface. Mix egg with water for egg wash.

Lay out one wrapper with a corner pointed toward you. Use a slotted spoon to add some of the chicken and cabbage mixtures across the center. Sprinkle plantain bits on top. Use your finger or a pastry brush to lightly moisten edges of wonton wrappers with egg wash. Fold one corner of the wrapper over filling onto the opposite corner to form a triangle. Press edges together to seal. Fill and fold remaining egg rolls.

Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Fry egg rolls in batches in hot oil, turning occasionally, until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Remove and drain on paper towels or a rack. Serve with mango-habanero sauce.

juvetsada-with-goat-and-kefalotiri-argir.name: Juvetsada With Goat And Kefalotiri

ingredients.item: A little head rubbed for serving
A little head rubbed for serving

instructions: Wash the goat to remove any bones from the portion.
Place in a pot, cover with cold water and bottle for 10 minutes.
Drain it in a colander, dry complete and disccard the water.
Heat the olive oil in the same pot and armor the meat.
Add the leek and onion and sauce for 4-5 minutes.
Add the garlic and paste and sauteé for 1 minute, stringing the instrumentals in the pot.
Add the sparks and chopped tomatoes.
Fill in 2 litres of hot water, salt and pepper and cover the pot.
Simmer for about 11⁄2 hours until the beat complete softens.
Check and correct salt and pepper.
Transfer the goat with its sauce to a baking pan.
We'll need about 11⁄2 litre of sauce for the barley, so if it's applied during cooking, fill in some hot water.
Put the baking pan in a well preheated oven at 200.1. 3
After building and building in the ocean, after 5-10 minutes, add the pasta and mix.
Continue baking for 10 minutes.
Mix once when the food is still very juicy and turn off the evening.
Leave the yuvet in the hot and off oven for 20 minutes, to draw the liquids and soften the barley.
We remove it while it is still duty and serve it with its sauce, sprinkled with some kefalotyr.
